---
title: "Full CV"
toc: true
---

::: {.section-block}
## Education & Careers

**Hanyang University, HYU** (Seoul, Republic of Korea)\
Ph.D. candidate in Electronic Engineering (GPA 4.3/4.5) · Mar 2024 – Aug 2027 (est)\
*Semiconductor Nanophotonics Laboratory*\
Advisor: Prof. Mun Seok Jeong

**Dongguk University, DGU** (Seoul, Republic of Korea)\
M.S. in Solid-state Physics (GPA 4.5/4.5) · Sep 2022 – Feb 2024\
*Advanced Physics on Electromaterial Laboratory*\
Advisor: Prof. Kwun-Bum Chung\
Thesis: *"Performance Control of InSnZnO/InGaZnO Multi-Channel-Layered Transistors with Defect Analysis of Amorphous Oxide Semiconductors"*

B.S. in Physics (GPA 4.2/4.5) · Mar 2016 – Aug 2022\
Military Service in the Republic of Korea · Jun 2019 – Apr 2021\
Undergraduate researcher in Advanced Physics on Electromaterial Laboratory · Jul 2021 – Aug 2022
:::

::: {.section-block}
## Scholarships & Awards

| Award | Affiliation | Year |
|:------|:------------|-----:|
| HY-BrainKorea G3 2nd Award | Hanyang University | 2025 |
| IC-PBL Graduate Championship 3rd Award | Hanyang University | 2024 |
| Dongguk Future Talent Scholarship | Dongguk University | 2022, 2023 |
| Physics Department Alumni Scholarship | Dongguk University | 2022, 2023 |
| Dongguk 1.1.1 Scholarship | Dongguk University | 2021 |
:::

::: {.section-block}
## Skills

| Category | Details |
|:---------|:--------|
| **Spectroscopy** | Optical spectroscopy (Raman, Photoluminescence), Ellipsometry, Deep-level transient spectroscopy (DLTS), Tip-enhanced nanospectroscopy |
| **Machine Learning** | Physics-based data analysis, Deep learning for experimental data, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) |
| **Simulation** | Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) simulations, Inverse design with adjoint optimization |
| **Others** | Electrical characterization (I-V, etc), X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS), Photolithography, Sputtering |
:::

::: {.section-block}
## Research

My research focuses on quantitative defect spectroscopy and experimental data analysis for two-dimensional (2D) materials and oxide semiconductors. I aim to extract physically meaningful parameters from electrical and optical measurements and relate them directly to material properties and device behavior, with an emphasis on interpretability and reproducibility.

### 1. Defect & Optical Spectroscopy

- Quantitative analysis of intrinsic and extrinsic defects in 2D materials and oxide semiconductors.
- Extraction of defect energy levels, densities, and carrier dynamics using Deep Level Transient Spectroscopy (DLTS) and Photo-Induced Current Transient Spectroscopy (PICTS).
- Raman/Photoluminescence (PL) spectroscopy for probing defect states, doping, strain, and phase transitions.
- X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS) and spectroscopic ellipsometry (SE) for chemical composition, bonding states, and optical properties.
- Tip-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy (TERS) for spatially resolved defect and structural inhomogeneity analysis beyond the diffraction limit.

### 2. Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) for Experimental Data

- Development of analysis pipelines for spectroscopic and electrical datasets, including preprocessing, feature extraction, modeling, and validation.
- XAI to identify physically relevant data features.
- Connection of AI-extracted features to defect-related mechanisms and material properties under data-limited experimental conditions.

### 3. Advanced Electronic & Photonic Devices

- Fabrication and characterization of electronic devices based on 2D materials and oxide semiconductors, including logic and neuromorphic devices.
- Quantitative analysis of device electrical responses (e.g., I–V characteristics and transient behavior) to identify dominant transport and trapping mechanisms.
- Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) simulations to evaluate optical responses and electric-field distributions in nano- to micro-scale structures, supporting the design and analysis of photonic components.

My work integrates defect-resolved spectroscopy, explainable analysis of experimental data, and device-level characterization to obtain quantitatively grounded insight into solid-state materials. The primary objective is to extract physically meaningful parameters from measurements and connect them directly to material behavior and device performance.
:::

::: {.section-block .pub-list}
## Publications

{{< include data/publications.md >}}

:::

::: {.section-block}
## Research Fellowship

**"Deep-Level Transient Spectroscopy and Nanospectroscopy for Ultrafine Defect Analysis Using Explainable AI in 2D Quantum Semiconductors"**\
Basic Science Research Program through the National Research Foundation of Korea (NRF)\
Funded by the Ministry of Education · Sep 2025 – Aug 2027 · 50M KRW
:::
